old_claim.note: Municipal Water Supply provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No dependency remains because municipal drinking-water distribution is a different water-system scope from navigation locks for boats.

source_shape.source_locator: Definition describing a lock as a gated basin in a canal, river, or dock for raising or lowering boats.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source defines the technology as navigation infrastructure for moving boats between water levels.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That definition contradicts the old municipal-water-supply edge: drinking-water conveyance is not a prerequisite for a boat-navigation chamber.

ontology_before: Canal lock gates inherited a water-supply source and municipal-water prerequisite.
ontology_after: Canal lock gates are modeled as canal-navigation infrastructure, separate from municipal water supply.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: The graph no longer conflates canal locks with municipal water-distribution systems.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to a water-supply lock or valve rather than a canal-navigation lock.
A source showed the first scoped canal locks came from municipal drinking-water infrastructure.
A separate urban water-control node were introduced where municipal water supply is correctly required.
short_reason: Municipal water supply is a different water-system scope from navigation canal locks.
